refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not include multiple obligations or implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to ensure packet integrity
Prioritize critical teleoperation data packets to reduce effective packet loss
Establish redundant communication channels to mitigate packet loss impact
Monitor network quality in real-time and adjust teleoperation parameters accordingly
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on real-time network monitoring data
Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control logic
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on adapting communication parameters to meet packet loss requirements, which logically isolates the communication quality function. Potential coupling arises if teleoperation control logic tightly integrates with communication adjustments, but this can be managed with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to allow adaptive protocol effectiveness
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with available network infrastructure
Real-time monitoring data is accurate and timely to enable protocol adaptation
Operational policies do not restrict dynamic communication adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of network conditions and specify monitoring requirements to ensure the adaptive protocol can function effectively under all expected scenarios.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network and communication management components responsible for implementing adaptive communication protocols to maintain packet loss within limits.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving network packets, essential for adaptive communication protocol operation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs communication status and performance metrics to other system modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since protocol logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol; would require significant diffusion of protocol logic.
Teleoperation depends on communication but protocol implementation here would cause coupling and diffusion.
Safety Services may monitor communication but does not implement adaptive protocol; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive communication protocol DP, as it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
Changes in communication standards may require updates across multiple modules if interfaces are not well defined.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive communication protocol to maintain packet loss within limits' directly addresses the FR requirement to support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The DP logically solves the FR by focusing on adaptive protocol implementation to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may introduce coupling if interfaces are not carefully managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and centralizes the adaptive communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion risk. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, indicating that the protocol logic is well contained within NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module is clearly defined as the implementation boundary for the DP, with well-specified interfaces to external modules, supporting clear ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define and document interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and ensure modularity.
Establish a process to update the NGHE Solution module interfaces and protocol logic in response to changes in communication standards to avoid cross-module impact.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The DP logically solves the FR, the IM contains the DP with manageable diffusion risks, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, the recommended revisions focus on interface documentation and process establishment rather than further decomposition. Therefore, no further sub-FRs are necessary at this time.

revise.why: To explicitly define and document interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and ensure modularity, and to establish a process for updating NGHE Solution module interfaces and protocol logic in response to communication standard changes.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol implemented in the NGHE Solution module sufficiently meets the packet loss requirements under all operational conditions.
Interfaces between NGHE Solution and other modules can be clearly defined and maintained to manage coupling risks.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may introduce coupling if interfaces are not carefully managed.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface definitions and update processes for NGHE Solution module.
